title: Rare Earth Elements (REE)
focus: Applications, global reserves, and distribution of REE in India.
rationale: REE is a critical mineral topic for UPSC, especially regarding China's dominance and India's monazite sand reserves.
sectionExcerpt: Rare Earth Elements (REE) are used in electronics, renewable energy, and defence. China dominates global production (over 2/3rd) and supply chain (85%), holding 49% of world reserves, followed by Brazil (23%) and India (8%). In India, REE reserves are mostly found in monazite sands in coastal states like Tamil Nadu, Kerala, Andhra Pradesh, and Odisha.
evidenceBullets: China controls more than 85% of the global REE supply chain and holds 49% of world reserves.
India holds 8% of global REE reserves.
India's REE reserves are primarily found in monazite sands.
Coastal states like Tamil Nadu, Kerala, Andhra Pradesh, and Odisha have the most rare earth deposits in India.

title: Nanotechnology Basics and Applications
focus: Core concepts of nanotechnology and its diverse applications across sectors.
rationale: Foundational science topic for UPSC Prelims, focusing on the nanoscale definition and practical applications in agriculture, health, and environment.
sectionExcerpt: Nanotechnology involves working with materials at the nanoscale (1 to 100 nm). At this scale, materials exhibit unusual properties depending on shape, size, and surface characteristics. Applications include nanofertilizers in agriculture, targeted drug delivery in health, nanostructured solar cells in energy, and water purification in the environment. A major concern is the potential toxicity of nanoparticles.
evidenceBullets: Nanotechnology deals with materials ranging from 1 to 100 nm in size.
Nanomaterial properties depend on shape, size, surface characteristics, and inner structure.
Applications include targeted drug delivery, nanofertilizers, and water purification.
Cosmetics use nanoparticles of certain metallic oxides.

title: Nanozymes
focus: Definition, advantages, and recent developments of nanozymes.
rationale: Specific biotech/nanotech advancement with direct health applications, highly testable as a science and tech current affair.
sectionExcerpt: Nanozymes are nanomaterials with intrinsic enzyme-like properties. They offer advantages over natural enzymes, including high catalytic activity, low cost, and high stability. Researchers at IISc developed an artificial metal-based nanozyme (vanadium pentoxide or V2O5) to treat abnormal blood clotting caused by conditions like pulmonary thromboembolism (PTE).
evidenceBullets: Nanozymes are nanomaterials that possess intrinsic enzyme-like properties.
Compared to natural enzymes, nanozymes have high stability, low cost, and high catalytic activity.
IISc developed a vanadium pentoxide (V2O5) nanozyme to treat abnormal blood clotting (PTE).

title: Nano Fertilizers and Nano Sulphur
focus: Agricultural applications of nanotechnology including IFFCO's nano fertilizers and TERI's nano sulphur.
rationale: Agriculture-tech is a high-yield UPSC topic. The distinction between IFFCO's chemical nano fertilizers and TERI's biological nano sulphur is a prime candidate for statement-based questions.
sectionExcerpt: Nano fertilizers encapsulate nutrients within nanomaterials (100 nm or less) for controlled release and slow diffusion into soil. IFFCO, which launched Nano Liquid Urea (2021) and Nano-DAP (2023), is setting up its first overseas plant in Brazil. TERI scientists developed a green nano sulphur using biological agents (plant-promoting bacteria), which has antibacterial and insecticidal properties and increased mustard yield by 30-40%.
evidenceBullets: Nano fertilizers measure 100 nanometres or less and enable controlled nutrient release into the soil.
IFFCO launched the world's first Nano Liquid Urea in 2021 and Nano-DAP in 2023.
TERI's nano-sulphur is a green product utilizing biological agents like plant-promoting bacteria.
Nano-sulphur possesses antibacterial and insecticidal properties.

title: Metal-Organic Framework (MOF)
focus: Structure, properties, and significance of Metal-Organic Frameworks.
rationale: The 2025 Nobel Prize in Chemistry makes this a highly probable topic for Prelims. The structural composition (metal ions + organic molecules) is key.
sectionExcerpt: The 2025 Nobel Prize in Chemistry was awarded for the development of Metal-Organic Frameworks (MOFs). MOFs are special materials made of metal ions (acting as joints) and organic carbon-based molecules (acting as links). They join through chemical bonds to form 1D, 2D, or 3D network structures. These structures contain cavities, making the material highly porous.
evidenceBullets: The 2025 Nobel Prize in Chemistry was awarded for the development of Metal-Organic Frameworks (MOFs).
MOFs consist of metal ions acting as joints and organic molecules acting as links.
MOFs form network structures that can be one, two, or three-dimensional.
The presence of cavities makes MOF materials highly porous.
